body.title: weekly-retro 2026-07-05
body.text:
## Week ending 2026-07-05 (Friday extras run late on Sunday)

### Hypothesis confirmation rate
ZERO outcomes recorded this week despite very heavy shipping (~15 decisions 06-30..07-05, most with all tickets closed). This is the flag the retro exists to catch: the /outcome loop is NOT closing. We ship + file follow-ups but never record whether the hypothesis was confirmed — so the confirmation rate is unmeasurable, which is the same disease Fable's 2026-07-05 pressure-test found at the code level (metrics with no capturing query). The dev process is not yet dogfooding its own measurement discipline.

### Priority changes for next week
1. Close the outcome loop on the decisions where we already HAVE data: D-coding-loop-redesign-aider-survey (the funnel gave us live confirm-by: architect fix works, editor is the new bottleneck) and D-ds-inference-context-management (num_ctx necessary-not-sufficient). Run /outcome on both.
2. The new D-compiled-inference-proof-program is the structural fix — it institutionalizes measurement (self-model checker + metric-to-capture tracing = Fable Q5). Treat "every decision names a capturing query" as the standing lesson.

### Needs /outcome (shipped, no outcome recorded)
D-coding-loop-redesign-aider-survey-2026-07-04 (have data), D-ds-inference-context-management-2026-07-04 (have data), plus the 06-30..07-03 batch (worker-pool, granny-pull-dispatch, swarm-location-independent, env-creds-cleanup, ds-builder-leveling, inference-domain-routing, teach-on-miss). Several 06-25..06-28 decisions are >7d overdue.
